name: Velarix Web Source of Truth
purpose: Canonical implementation contract for the Velarix website in console/. This file defines the approved framework, libraries, runtime assumptions, architecture rules, performance budgets, and design-system integration points. It is intentionally strict so a junior developer can build without inventing stack decisions.
productGoals: Ship a fast, dark-mode-only marketing and product web presence.
Optimize for static delivery first, with dynamic rendering used only where the product truly needs it.
Preserve a precise, industrial UI that maps directly to BRAND_SPEC.md.
Stay Vercel-native so preview deploys, analytics, edge delivery, and operational tooling work with minimal custom infrastructure.

framework.name: Next.js
renderingModel: Client Components only for browser interactivity, animation control, or stateful UI
Streaming and Suspense allowed where they improve perceived performance
why: Next.js App Router is the default architecture for modern React applications and is the recommended path for new work.
The App Router provides layouts, Server Components, streaming, and colocated data access patterns that match a high-performance 2026 web stack.
The Vercel platform has first-class operational support for Next.js.
rules: Do not use the Pages Router.
Do not create a custom Express or Fastify server.
Prefer server rendering and static prerendering over client-side data fetching.
Use Route Handlers only when a backend endpoint is actually needed.

react.name: React
rules: Assume Server Components first.
Use Client Components only when required by event handling, browser APIs, Motion hooks, or client state.
Keep client boundaries shallow and intentional.

styling.name: Tailwind CSS
styling.configurationModel: CSS-first Tailwind v4 configuration with theme variables mapped to the Brand Spec tokens

rules: Map Inter to the global font-sans token.
Map Lora to the global font-serif token.
Use serif only for headline accent words and editorial emphasis.
All visual tokens must originate from BRAND_SPEC.md and be exposed as CSS custom properties.
Tailwind utility usage is preferred over ad hoc CSS modules for standard spacing, layout, typography, borders, and color application.
Component-specific CSS is allowed only for advanced visual systems, layered backgrounds, complex masks, or animation primitives that are awkward in utilities alone.
Do not introduce a second styling system such as styled-components, Emotion, or Chakra.

uiLibrary.name: shadcn/ui
uiLibrary.model: Open-code component baseline, copied into the codebase and customized locally
rules: Use shadcn/ui as the accessibility and composition starting point, not as an untouchable preset.
Every imported component must be restyled to match BRAND_SPEC.md before production use.
Keep the component API surface close to upstream unless the Velarix design system requires a local opinion.
Prefer shadcn primitives for dialog, sheet, popover, tooltip, dropdown, tabs, accordion, and form controls.

animations.name: Framer Motion
usage: Shared layout transitions where state changes need visual continuity
rules: Motion must support the brand's deterministic feel. No playful bounce or exaggerated spring defaults.
Prefer transform and opacity animation paths for performance.
Gate non-essential motion behind reduced-motion checks.
Use layout animations only when the spatial continuity is materially helpful.

icons.name: lucide-react
rules: Lucide is the default icon source for the site.
Do not mix icon families in production UI.
Use consistent sizes and stroke alignment across navigation, buttons, and data views.
Use Vercel preview deployments for every pull request.
Assume global edge delivery for static assets.
Use Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights in production.
Keep the app compatible with Vercel Functions and Route Handlers without custom server infrastructure.

allowedSupportingPackages: next-themes only if used to hard-lock dark mode without exposing a theme switch
disallowedPatterns: No Material UI, Chakra UI, Mantine, or Ant Design.
No dual animation systems such as GSAP plus Motion unless a later spec explicitly approves it.
No client-side router abstractions that bypass Next.js navigation.

conventions: Use route groups to separate marketing, docs, and application concerns if needed.
Use colocated loading, error, and not-found files where route behavior demands it.
Keep root layout minimal and global.

suggestedFolders.components/brand: Velarix-specific marketing and visual system components.
suggestedFolders.lib: Pure utility functions, config helpers, token maps, and server-safe helpers.
suggestedFolders.styles: Global CSS, token definitions, and advanced visual layers.
suggestedFolders.content: Structured local content if MDX or JSON-backed content is later adopted.

clientTriggers: Motion hooks such as useScroll or useTransform
rules: Never mark an entire page as client-side without a specific reason.
Push data shaping and heavy work to the server side where possible.
Keep client bundles small by isolating interactive islands.
Marketing content should be statically generated whenever possible.
Use server-side fetch in Server Components for dynamic content that must remain fresh.
Cache intentionally. Avoid uncontrolled no-store usage.
If CMS integration is added later, wrap access behind typed server utilities.
Do not introduce a CMS before the site structure stabilizes.
Keep copy, navigation, social metadata, and repetitive page content in typed config objects.
Promote to MDX only if editorial workflow becomes a real requirement.

implementationRules: Translate every color, radius, spacing, and typography rule into CSS custom properties first.
Expose those variables to Tailwind v4 theme tokens.
Override shadcn component defaults so buttons, inputs, cards, dialogs, and navigation all inherit Velarix tokens by default.
Do not hardcode brand values deep inside individual components when a token should exist.

componentDefaults.button: 4px radius, visible 1px border, compact height, deterministic hover states
componentDefaults.card: 6px radius, always bordered, dark flat surface, no soft marketing shadow
componentDefaults.input: 4px radius, dark fill, strong focus ring, muted placeholder
componentDefaults.dialog: 6px radius, elevated surface, bordered shell, restrained overlay scrim
componentDefaults.table: Dense spacing, sharp separators, subdued zebra only if readability requires it

principles: Perceived speed matters as much as raw speed.
Every page should feel immediate on a cold visit.
Animation is never allowed to become the largest performance cost.
rules: Prefer static rendering for top-level marketing pages.
Use next/font for local or self-hosted font loading.
Use next/image where appropriate, but do not force it for tiny decorative assets if plain img is simpler and faster.
Keep hero visuals GPU-friendly by favoring transforms, opacity, masks, and gradients over expensive filters.
Audit bundle impact before adding third-party libraries.

forbiddenPatterns: large blur animations on every frame
looping motion that distracts from reading
autoplay sequences that cannot be reduced or paused when they become dominant
reducedMotionPolicy: Replace movement with opacity changes or static states where possible.
Do not remove information when motion is disabled.

rules: Use App Router metadata APIs for title, description, Open Graph, and Twitter metadata.
Generate per-page metadata from typed config where practical.
Keep canonical URLs explicit once production domains are defined.
Generate sitemap and robots rules inside the Next.js app when the route map stabilizes.
All interactive elements must be keyboard reachable and visibly focused.
Dark-mode contrast must meet WCAG AA minimums.
Semantics come before div-based composition.
Icons used alone must have accessible labels.
Motion and color must never be the sole carrier of meaning.

minimum: Build must pass on Vercel preview before merge.
recommended: Playwright for critical route smoke tests.
Visual review in preview deployments for every major UI change.
Lighthouse and Web Vitals review before public launch.

deliveryWorkflow.branching: Feature branches with Vercel preview deploys
pullRequestChecklist: Matches BRAND_SPEC.md tokens and component logic
Performance impact checked for hero and nav changes

bootstrapChecklist: Create a Next.js App Router application in console/ using TypeScript.
Install Tailwind CSS v4 and configure theme variables in CSS.
Install shadcn/ui and copy components locally.
Install motion, lucide-react, class-variance-authority, clsx, and tailwind-merge.
Add Inter and Lora through next/font or local self-hosted files.
Encode BRAND_SPEC.md as global CSS custom properties and Tailwind theme tokens.
Create customized base primitives before building any page sections.
Enable Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights when the app is deployed.

ifUnsure: Choose the simpler solution that preserves performance and brand precision.
Prefer built-in Next.js and platform capabilities over custom infrastructure.
Prefer local component ownership over black-box UI packages.
When a stack choice conflicts with BRAND_SPEC.md, the brand contract wins unless a new spec explicitly changes it.
